obligations.note: One-shot continuation reuse is still enforced by the stack runtime implementation.
Missing active prompt detection still comes from the stack runtime context.
Thread-affinity checks still live in the stack runtime owner.
Destroyed-runtime misuse still depends on the stack runtime state machine.
Nested capture diagonality is still enforced by the stack runtime control engine.
Non-diagonal completion is still guarded by the stack runtime implementation.
The runtime-positive survey fixture now executes through the lowered runtime seam instead of the raw stack runtime.
Most one-shot survey fixtures prove public protocol shape at compile time only.
Compile-fail misuse fixtures already target public API and type-shape constraints rather than runtime execution.
Prompt shell compactness is already independent of the runtime backend.
Runtime option fields remain source-visible compatibility fields even though stack sizing now uses internal defaults.
Default build/test wiring still adds stack-switch assembly files for the shipped runtime path.
